Implementation approach: We will use Python as the primary programming language due to its extensive library support and ease of use for scripting. PyUSB will be used for USB communication with the devices, and PySerial for any serial communication needs. For the GUI, we will use PyQt5 to create a user-friendly interface that supports dual-window operations. The network messaging will be handled using the ZeroMQ library, which is suitable for high-performance asynchronous messaging. The software will be modular, allowing easy integration and control of each device.
Program call flow:
sequenceDiagram
    participant M as Main
    participant UC as USBControl
    participant NM as NetworkMessaging
    participant G as GUI
    participant SE as ScriptExecutor
    participant DI as DeviceInterface
    M->>G: start_interface()
    G->>M: load_script(file_path)
    M->>SE: execute_script(script)
    SE->>UC: initialize_device(device_id)
    UC->>DI: control_mks_pdr2000()
    UC->>DI: control_watlow()
    M->>NM: send_message(ip, message)
    NM-->>M: receive_message()
    G->>M: manual_control()
    M->>UC: read_analog_input(channel)
    UC-->>M: return value
    M->>UC: write_analog_output(channel, value)
Anything UNCLEAR: Clarification needed on the specific commands and protocols used by the mks type pdr 2000 and watlow controllers. Additionally, details on the expected format and content of network messages would be helpful.
